step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the previous conclusion that option C is the incorrect statement regarding acute leukemia in children. One additional reason supporting this is that while both ALL and AML occur frequently in children, ALL remains more commonly diagnosed across various age groups including adolescents. Hence, stating otherwise would be misleading especially since statistical evidence consistently shows higher occurrence percentages associated with ALL relative to AML among young patients.

raw_response:
I agree with the previous conclusion that option C is the incorrect statement regarding acute leukemia in children. One additional reason supporting this is that while both ALL and AML occur frequently in children, ALL remains more commonly diagnosed across various age groups including adolescents. Hence, stating otherwise would be misleading especially since statistical evidence consistently shows higher occurrence percentages associated with ALL relative to AML among young patients.
